question: For Button, Checkbox, Field, and Popover, does the generic compiler produce acceptable native Vue/Svelte/Solid components faster and cheaper than expert hand-maintained ports?

A.name: generic-compiler
A.description: Existing spike: canonical source frontend -> tracer -> part-first IR -> generic per-target lowerers -> native harness -> differential verifier.
A.constraint: Target lowerers MUST NOT contain component-specific branches or component IDs in control flow.

B.name: hand-port-baseline
B.description: Expert-competent native Vue/Svelte/Solid implementations of the same four components using canonical Kumo CSS/tokens, no React runtime, no generator.

required: meaningful-part geometry matches within 1 CSS pixel

exactPrimitives.rule: zero differing pixels after declared nondeterminism handling

compositeAndAnimated.rule: pixel mismatch <= 0.25% AND no meaningful-part geometry diff > 1 CSS pixel; invisible portal/focus wrappers excluded

unseenExtension.description: After both lanes pass the initial four, introduce one previously-unfrozen change and measure marginal work in both lanes.

promoteCriteria: four proving components pass product parity in all three targets
unseen extension requires no lowerer change
compiler marginal effort >= 2x cheaper than hand ports
hybridCriteria: Popover needs target-owned portal/focus adapter via explicit measured plugin API (not hidden component branches)
stopCriteria: generated code harder to own than hand code
